name: unstructured
description: Partition documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, images, and more) into structured JSON elements for RAG ingestion via the Unstructured Partition API

envVars.name: UNSTRUCTURED_API_KEY
envVars.description: Unstructured API key, sent as the unstructured-api-key request header

tools.name: Partition Document
tools.description: Parse one local document into structured JSON elements (titles, narrative text, tables, lists) with optional chunking for RAG. The file content is uploaded as the multipart/form-data 'files' field together with the other fields; remote URLs are not accepted — fetch the file first and pass its content

files.description: Base64-encoded document content, sent decoded as the binary 'files' part of the multipart request

file_name.description: Filename for the uploaded document, e.g. report.pdf; the extension helps Unstructured detect the file type

strategy.description: Partitioning strategy: auto (default), fast, hi_res (layout-aware, best for tables), ocr_only, or vlm (requires vlm_model and vlm_model_provider)

vlm_model.description: VLM model to use, e.g. claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 or gpt-4o; required when strategy is vlm

vlm_model_provider.description: Provider of the VLM model, e.g. anthropic, openai, bedrock, or vertexai; required when strategy is vlm

content_type.description: MIME type hint for the uploaded file, e.g. application/pdf

chunking_strategy.description: Chunk elements for RAG: basic, by_title, by_page, or by_similarity; omit to return raw elements

max_characters.description: Hard maximum chunk size in characters when chunking (default 500)

languages.description: Document language codes to improve OCR/partitioning, e.g. ["eng"]

coordinates.description: Include element bounding-box coordinates in the output (default false)

prompts.title: Parse a document for RAG
prompts.prompt: Partition the document I provide with Unstructured using by_title chunking and summarize the resulting elements.

prompts.title: Extract tables
prompts.prompt: Partition my document with the hi_res strategy and extract the tables it contains.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Unstructured API Setup

steps.title: Create an Unstructured account
steps.description: Sign up at https://platform.unstructured.io — new accounts include a free trial of the serverless API, enough for testing without billing.

steps.title: Get an API key
steps.description: In the Unstructured UI, open the API Keys section, create a key, and copy it.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Add UNSTRUCTURED_API_KEY=... to your .env file. Requests authenticate with the unstructured-api-key header (no Bearer prefix).

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the Partition Document tool with a small text or PDF file and strategy auto. A 401 means the key is wrong; a 422 usually means the files field is missing or unreadable.

notes: The Partition endpoint processes one local file per request and does not fetch remote URLs — download the file first and pass its content
Your account may be issued a dedicated API URL; the default https://api.unstructuredapp.io/general/v0/general works for standard serverless accounts
hi_res and vlm strategies are slower and cost more per page than fast/auto but handle complex layouts and tables much better
Use chunking_strategy by_title with max_characters tuned to your embedding model for RAG-ready chunks
